output: A TTL re-verification job (TypeScript) committed via PR, with tests covering expiry detection, re-verification, and status-change pulling.
context: LoC rights metadata can change after an item is cleared. Every manifest carries a determination TTL; items whose TTL has expired must be re-verified against current LoC metadata before re-publication. A changed rights status must pull the item from the cleared set and log it. Without this job, stale clears could be re-published violating current rights. Depends on prov-001. See PLAN.md (Risks row for stale clear, Sustainability).
title: Re-verification (determination TTL) job for stale clears
acceptanceCriteria: The job reads manifests of all cleared items and identifies those whose TTL expiry has passed.
For each expired item, current LoC rights metadata is re-fetched using the polite client honoring all rate limits and cache.
The rights-gate is re-run; items still eligible get an updated manifest with a fresh TTL.
Items whose rights status has changed to ineligible or needs-review are pulled from the cleared set and recorded in the quarantine ledger with reason ttl-status-changed.
The job is idempotent: re-running does not double-process or duplicate records.
pnpm build and pnpm test and pnpm lint pass; a test with a simulated status-change verifies the pull logic.
objective: Implement the TTL re-verification job that identifies cleared items with an expired determination TTL, re-fetches current LoC rights metadata via the polite client, re-runs the rights-gate, updates manifests for still-eligible items, and pulls items with a changed rights status from the cleared set with a log entry.
